criteria: The agent has read Section 2.1 completely to understand the SFT objective (Equation 1-2) and importance sampling framework (Equations 3-5).

The agent has read Section 2.2 completely to understand the ATLANTIS method, including the derivation of the importance-weighted loss and the proportionality assumption used to estimate the sampling ratio.
The agent has read Algorithm 1 completely to understand the full training procedure: precomputing influence weights from p_b^L and p_b^S before training, then applying weighted loss each step.
The agent has read Section 2.3 to understand the concept of influence weights (p_r(y|x)/p_b^S(y|x)) and the relationship to proxy-tuning (Equation 8-9).
The agent has read Table 1 to understand the specific model triplets (p_b^L, p_b^S, p_r) for each model family used in experiments.
The agent has read Section 3.2 to understand all evaluation benchmarks: Open LLM Leaderboard 2 (BBH, GPQA, IFEval, MATH-Hard, MMLU-Pro, MUSR), TruthfulQA, MT-Bench, AlpacaEval, and Arena-Hard-Auto.
The agent has read Table 2 to understand the main experimental results comparing ATLANTIS vs SFT across Llama2-13B, Qwen2-7B, and Gemma2-9B on all benchmarks.
The agent has read Table 3 to understand the data selection experiment settings and results with IFD and Superfiltering at sample rates 0.05, 0.10, and 0.15.
The agent has read Table 4 to understand the ATLANTIS-cross experiment design and the results of cross-model-family influence weight transfer.
The agent has read Table 5 to understand the large-scale model experiments with Qwen2-72B and Gemma2-27B.

The agent has read Appendix A (Table 6) to understand all implementation details including hardware, training steps, batch size, learning rate, gradient accumulation, optimizer, scheduler, and DeepSpeed configuration.
The plan includes implementing the standard SFT loss (Equation 2) as the baseline training objective.
The plan includes implementing the ATLANTIS loss function (Equation 7) by multiplying log p_b^L(y|x) by the precomputed ratio p_b^L(y|x)/p_b^S(y|x).
The plan includes precomputing per-sample influence weights W = {p_b^L(y_i|x_i)/p_b^S(y_i|x_i) * p_r(y_i|x_i)} over the entire training dataset before the training loop begins.
The plan includes setting up three model roles for each experiment: large base model p_b^L, small base model p_b^S, and reference (finetuned) model p_r as specified in Table 1.
The plan includes loading and preparing the OpenHermes-2.5 training dataset for instruction tuning.

The plan includes all training hyperparameters from Table 6 differentiated by model size: training steps (60K/30K/15K), learning rate (2e-5/5e-6/5e-7), batch size (2/1/1), and gradient accumulation steps (8/8/4).
The plan includes configuring DeepSpeed ZeRO Stage 3 with both optimizer offload and parameter offload enabled.
The plan includes setting up lm-evaluation-harness to evaluate all six Open LLM Leaderboard 2 tasks (BBH, GPQA, IFEval, MATH-Hard, MMLU-Pro, MUSR) and TruthfulQA (MC1, MC2).
The plan includes configuring MT-Bench evaluation in both single-mode (scoring) and pairwise mode (win rate), AlpacaEval win rate evaluation, and Arena-Hard-Auto score evaluation.
The plan includes conducting data selection experiments with IFD and Superfiltering at sample rates 0.05, 0.10, and 0.15 for Qwen2-7B and Gemma2-9B.
The plan includes implementing ATLANTIS-cross by computing influence weights from a different model family than the one being finetuned (Qwen2 weights for Gemma2 training and vice versa).
The plan includes running scale experiments with Qwen2-72B (p_b^L=72B Base, p_b^S=7B Base, p_r=7B-Instruct) and Gemma2-27B (p_b^L=27B Base, p_b^S=9B Base, p_r=9B-it).
The plan includes implementing proxy-tuning (Equation 9) as a comparison baseline by logit manipulation: softmax(s_b^L + s_r - s_b^S).
The standard SFT loss (Equation 2) has been implemented as: L(theta) = -sum_{x,y} p_d(y|x) log p_theta(y|x), computing cross-entropy only on output tokens y.
The code computes token-level log probabilities from the large base model p_b^L and small base model p_b^S over all training samples (x_i, y_i) prior to training.
The per-sample influence weight W_i = p_b^L(y_i|x_i)/p_b^S(y_i|x_i) * p_r(y_i|x_i) has been precomputed over the full training dataset and stored before the training loop.
The ATLANTIS loss function (Equation 7) has been implemented as L = -sum_{i in B} W_i/|B| * log p_theta(y_i|x_i), where W_i is the precomputed fixed weight for each sample.

The proportionality assumption from Section 2.2 has been correctly implemented: p*(y|x)/p_r(y|x) is approximated by p_b^L(y|x)/p_b^S(y|x), derived from the proxy assumption p*(y|x)/p_b^L(y|x) ∝ p_r(y|x)/p_b^S(y|x).

The training loop from Algorithm 1 has been implemented: initialize p_theta_0 from p_b^L, iterate M steps fetching batches B and corresponding precomputed weights W_B, update via gradient of weighted loss.
The influence weight visualization (Figure 2) has been implemented to show similar weight distributions across Gemma-2-2B, Gemma-2-9B, Llama-2-7B, Qwen2-1.5B, Qwen2-7B models.
The Llama2 model triplet has been correctly loaded: p_b^L = Llama2-13B Base, p_b^S = Llama2-7B Base, p_r = Llama2-7B-chat.
The Qwen2-7B model triplet has been correctly loaded: p_b^L = Qwen2-7B Base, p_b^S = Qwen2-1.5B Base, p_r = Qwen2-1.5B-Instruct.
The Gemma2-9B model triplet has been correctly loaded: p_b^L = Gemma2-9B Base, p_b^S = Gemma2-2B Base, p_r = Gemma2-2B-it.
The OpenHermes-2.5 dataset has been loaded with instruction-tuning format (chat template applied) for computing token-level log probabilities and model training.
The learning rates from Table 6 have been correctly implemented by model size: 2e-5 for models ≤13B, 5e-6 for 27B, 5e-7 for 72B.
The training steps from Table 6 have been correctly implemented by model size: 60K for models ≤13B, 30K for 27B, 15K for 72B.
The per-device batch size from Table 6 has been correctly implemented: 2 for models ≤13B, 1 for 27B and 72B.
The gradient accumulation steps from Table 6 have been correctly implemented: 8 for models ≤27B, 4 for 72B.
The AdamW optimizer has been configured with random seed 0 for reproducibility.
The learning rate scheduler has been implemented with linear warmup and linear decay: warmup steps of 6K for ≤13B, 3K for 27B, and 1.5K for 72B.
DeepSpeed ZeRO Stage 3 has been configured with optimizer_offload=True and parameter_offload=True to enable training of large models.
The correct GPU hardware configuration has been used: 16×A800 for models ≤13B, 32×A800 for 27B, 64×A800 for 72B.
The Python package versions have been set correctly: accelerate==0.30.0, deepspeed==0.13.5, torch==2.1.0+cu118, transformers==4.43.4 for Gemma2 and 4.40.2 for others.
The lm-evaluation-harness evaluation pipeline has been implemented to run all six Open LLM Leaderboard 2 tasks (BBH, GPQA, IFEval, MATH-Hard, MMLU-Pro, MUSR) on trained models.
The TruthfulQA evaluation has been implemented with lm-evaluation-harness to report both MC1 and MC2 accuracy metrics.
The MT-Bench evaluation has been implemented in both single mode (LLM scoring) and pairwise mode (win rate against a baseline) to match the paper's reported metrics.
The AlpacaEval evaluation has been implemented to report easy and hard win rates (AlpacaEval-Easy and AlpacaEval-Hard) against a fixed baseline model.
The Arena-Hard-Auto evaluation has been implemented to report model scores judged by an LLM on model responses.
The IFD-based data selection pipeline has been implemented to score and select training samples at sample rates 0.05, 0.10, and 0.15 from OpenHermes-2.5.
The Superfiltering-based data selection pipeline has been implemented to score and select training samples at sample rates 0.05, 0.10, and 0.15 from OpenHermes-2.5.
The ATLANTIS-cross variant has been implemented: computing influence weights using Qwen2-1.5B/1.5B-Instruct pair to finetune Gemma2-9B, and using Gemma2-2B/2B-it pair to finetune Qwen2-7B.
The proxy-tuning baseline (Equation 9) has been implemented as inference-time logit manipulation: softmax(s_b^L(y|x) + s_r(y|x) - s_b^S(y|x)) without any additional training.
The Qwen2-72B scale experiment model triplet has been correctly set up: p_b^L = Qwen2-72B Base, p_b^S = Qwen2-7B Base, p_r = Qwen2-7B-Instruct.
The Gemma2-27B scale experiment model triplet has been correctly set up: p_b^L = Gemma2-27B Base, p_b^S = Gemma2-9B Base, p_r = Gemma2-9B-it.
The influence weight distribution visualization has been implemented for Figure 4, plotting 2000 randomly selected samples to compare Qwen2-1.5B and Gemma2-2B weight distributions.
The main ATLANTIS and SFT training experiments on Llama2-13B, Qwen2-7B, and Gemma2-9B have been executed successfully without errors using the OpenHermes-2.5 dataset.
All evaluation benchmarks (Open LLM Leaderboard 2, TruthfulQA, MT-Bench, AlpacaEval, Arena-Hard-Auto) have been run successfully on all trained ATLANTIS and SFT model checkpoints.
The data selection experiments (IFD and Superfiltering at 0.05, 0.10, 0.15 sample rates) combined with ATLANTIS and SFT for Qwen2-7B and Gemma2-9B have been executed successfully.
The ATLANTIS-cross experiments for Qwen2-7B (using Gemma2 weights) and Gemma2-9B (using Qwen2 weights) have been executed successfully.
The large-scale experiments training Qwen2-72B and Gemma2-27B with both ATLANTIS and SFT have been executed successfully.
The proxy-tuning baseline experiment on Qwen2-7B has been executed successfully without requiring any model training.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: ATLANTIS consistently outperforms SFT on Open LLM Leaderboard 2 for Llama2-13B (33.50 vs 32.37), Qwen2-7B (38.19 vs 36.22), and Gemma2-9B (33.86 vs 33.51).

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: ATLANTIS significantly improves MT-Bench pairwise win rates over SFT for Llama2-13B (21.56 vs 19.06), Qwen2-7B (26.25 vs 25.63), and Gemma2-9B (27.50 vs 20.31).
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: ATLANTIS improves TruthfulQA MC2 scores for Llama2-13B (54.07 vs 53.36) and Gemma2-9B (55.23 vs 52.85), and MC1 for Gemma2-9B (39.41 vs 36.47).

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: ATLANTIS improves AlpacaEval pairwise win rates for Llama2-13B (7.80 vs 6.81) and Gemma2-9B (6.92 vs 6.28), and Arena-Hard-Auto scores for Llama2-13B (5.4 vs 3.9).

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 3: ATLANTIS consistently improves over plain SFT when combined with IFD data selection on Qwen2-7B across all sample rates (e.g., TruthfulQA MC2: 54.15 vs 52.59 at rate 0.05).
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 3: for Gemma2-9B, ATLANTIS with no data selection outperforms most IFD and Superfiltering configurations (TruthfulQA MC2: 55.23 is the highest).

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 4: ATLANTIS-cross (Qwen2 weights for Gemma2) achieves competitive or better results than same-structure ATLANTIS for Gemma2-9B TruthfulQA MC2 (55.40 vs 55.23) and MT-Bench single (7.31 vs 6.98).

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 4: ATLANTIS-cross (Gemma2 weights for Qwen2) causes a drop in MT-Bench pairwise for Qwen2-7B (21.56 vs 26.25), showing the cross-structure transfer limitation.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 5: ATLANTIS improves Qwen2-72B on TruthfulQA MC1 (42.59 vs 41.62), MC2 (61.73 vs 61.07), and MT-Bench pairwise (36.88 vs 31.88) compared to SFT.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 5: ATLANTIS improves Gemma2-27B MT-Bench pairwise (32.50 vs 26.56) despite a slight TruthfulQA MC2 drop (59.30 vs 61.24).

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 7: proxy-tuning severely underperforms SFT on MT-Bench single (3.76 vs 7.44) and pairwise (7.91 vs 25.63) for Qwen2-7B, while ATLANTIS matches or exceeds SFT.

The influence weight distribution analysis (Figure 4) shows that Qwen2-1.5B produces a higher-peak, narrower-variance distribution while Gemma2-2B produces a wider-variance distribution, with 46.78% disagreement rate when using 1.0 as the threshold.
